description: Cross-print Galarian Mr. Mime mirror parallel to the wave 27 E3 Galarian Meowth pattern. RBC-037 (kirisAki art) is the earlier card-pool print; DAA-035 (Misa Tsutsui art) is the wave-22 IP-verified anchor with full Galarian-evolution-pair context (DAA-035 ↔ DAA-036 Mr. Rime adjacent-collector mirror). Both prints share identical flavor text — tap-dancing + ice-floor-as-barrier — and the same canonical Galar UK-modeled British-music-hall performance-art register reframing the Kantonian Psychic/Fairy mime into Galar's Ice/Psychic tap-dancer. Mirror edge plus frontmatter wiring on RBC-037 (added `characters: [galarian-regional-forms]`) plus appears_on extension on the character node plus a one-sentence body update in the character node's RBC-Mr.-Mime entry. Clean fanout integration; the character node now anchors both prints of three species in the cohort (Meowth 2 prints, Mr. Mime 2 prints, Mr. Rime 1 print plus the singleton Slowpoke / Darumaka / Farfetch'd).
citations: [PokemonTCG.io API: swsh2-37 (Galarian Mr. Mime RBC); swsh3-35 (Galarian Mr. Mime DAA)]
[Pokejungle.net: Galarian Mr. Mime and Mr. Rime Origin of Species]
files_modified: cards/pokemon/darkness-ablaze/035-189-galarian-mr-mime.md (already had Connections section; left untouched per non-duplication discipline — the DAA side's existing bullet already covers the cohort context; the new cross-print mirror is documented from the RBC side and via the character-node appears_on extension)
footnote: DAA-035 already had a Connections section covering its in-set DAA-035 ↔ DAA-036 evolution mirror. Adding a second bullet to DAA-035 for the cross-print pointer would have been the strictly-symmetric mirror move; I chose to anchor the cross-print citation on the RBC side and via the character-node body update instead, since the cohort node now structurally captures both prints via appears_on. If a future Edgelord pass wants strict bullet-parity on the DAA side, the bullet text is ready in the RBC card's Connections section.
